item.title: FM Halkidis Controversy; Violent Weekend
item.ns0:title: FM Halkidis Controversy; Violent Weekend
item.description:
Tonight on Our News, the political showdown intensifies as the opposition demands the resignation of Finance Minister Michael Halkitis over controversial past associations, sparking a face-off between rival supporters in Parliament Square. We examine the critical recruitment challenges facing the Department of Corrections as officials struggle to find qualified male candidates to fill their ranks. Our team also looks into the sweeping reforms promised at the Public Parks and Public Beaches Authority following reports of massive overspending, and we bring you the latest on a string of weekend violence that has left families across the country in mourning. Stay tuned for these stories, a major milestone for the National School Breakfast Program, and your complete tropical weather outlook.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns1:encoded:
Tonight on Our News, the political showdown intensifies as the opposition demands the resignation of Finance Minister Michael Halkitis over controversial past associations, sparking a face-off between rival supporters in Parliament Square. We examine the critical recruitment challenges facing the Department of Corrections as officials struggle to find qualified male candidates to fill their ranks. Our team also looks into the sweeping reforms promised at the Public Parks and Public Beaches Authority following reports of massive overspending, and we bring you the latest on a string of weekend violence that has left families across the country in mourning. Stay tuned for these stories, a major milestone for the National School Breakfast Program, and your complete tropical weather outlook.
-----

item.ns0:summary:
Tonight on Our News, the political showdown intensifies as the opposition demands the resignation of Finance Minister Michael Halkitis over controversial past associations, sparking a face-off between rival supporters in Parliament Square. We examine the critical recruitment challenges facing the Department of Corrections as officials struggle to find qualified male candidates to fill their ranks. Our team also looks into the sweeping reforms promised at the Public Parks and Public Beaches Authority following reports of massive overspending, and we bring you the latest on a string of weekend violence that has left families across the country in mourning. Stay tuned for these stories, a major milestone for the National School Breakfast Program, and your complete tropical weather outlook.

item.ns0:author: Our News Bahamas
